skill.name: frontend-security-coder
skill.description: Secure frontend development guidance for XSS prevention, safe DOM handling, CSP configuration, input validation, and client-side security reviews.
skill.summary: Build and review frontend code with practical browser security controls.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Reviewed six static findings in SKILL.md and found no confirmed security issue. The detections are false positives caused by markdown backticks, browser security terminology, SAMEORIGIN text, and references to prevention topics. No evidence found of command execution, credential access, weak cryptography, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File Reference
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 26 uses markdown backticks around a documentation file path. It does not execute a Ruby command or shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious token is a markdown inline code span around a file path. No shell syntax, subprocess API, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 90 discusses secure JWT storage and localStorage versus sessionStorage. It is educational guidance, not code that reads browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a capability description about secure token storage. No JavaScript storage API call or data extraction path exists in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAMEORIGIN Header Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 75 references X-Frame-Options DENY and SAMEORIGIN. This is clickjacking protection guidance, not Windows SAM database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line names standard browser frame-protection header values. There is no filesystem path, Windows registry reference, or credential database operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography on Description
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 3 describes XSS prevention. It does not name or implement a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is YAML metadata text about frontend security. No cryptographic primitive, hash function, cipher, or crypto implementation appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography on Auditor Comparison
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 35 compares this skill with a security auditor role. It does not mention or implement weak cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes audit scope and security architecture reviews. No cryptographic algorithm or executable code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography on Redirect Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 83 describes open redirect prevention. It is navigation security guidance, not a cryptographic algorithm or crypto implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a prevention topic under secure redirects. No cryptographic primitive, key handling, or hashing behavior appears.

content.user_title: Secure Frontend Code Against Client-Side Attacks
content.value_statement: Frontend code often exposes users to XSS, unsafe redirects, and weak browser controls. This skill guides secure DOM handling, CSP setup, validation, and client-side defenses.
actual_capabilities: Guides safe DOM manipulation using textContent, secure element creation, and sanitization libraries.
Helps configure Content Security Policy directives, nonces, hashes, and reporting workflows.
Provides frontend validation patterns for forms, URLs, file uploads, and user-generated content.
Recommends clickjacking protections with frame controls, CSP frame-ancestors, and deployment safeguards.
Covers secure browser integrations, including SRI, Trusted Types, OAuth PKCE, and iframe sandboxing.
limitations: Does not perform automated penetration testing or runtime exploit validation.
Does not replace backend security controls, authentication checks, or server-side authorization.
Requires project-specific framework details to produce precise implementation changes.
The referenced implementation playbook file is not present in this package.

use_cases.title: Fix XSS Risk in User Content
use_cases.description: Help a frontend developer replace unsafe rendering with safer DOM APIs and sanitization controls.

use_cases.title: Review Browser Security Controls
use_cases.description: Help a security engineer review CSP, frame protections, URL validation, and third-party scripts.

use_cases.title: Harden a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Help a product team improve forms, redirects, token handling, and embedded widget security.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Frontend Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single component for common client-side issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend component for XSS, unsafe DOM updates, and insecure URL handling. Explain each risk and suggest safer changes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Safe Rendering
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me render user-generated content safely in my frontend app. Include sanitization choices, allowed content rules, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design a CSP Rollout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced deployment of browser policy controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased Content Security Policy rollout for this application. Include report-only testing, nonce or hash strategy, and compatibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Frontend Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of several client-side security controls together.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this frontend flow for XSS, clickjacking, redirect abuse, token storage, and third-party script risks. Prioritize fixes by impact.

output_examples.input: Review a comment renderer that inserts user text into the page.
output_examples.output: The response identifies unsafe rendering, recommends safer text insertion, suggests a sanitizer for rich text, and lists regression tests.

output_examples.input: Create a CSP for a React application using third-party analytics.
output_examples.output: The response proposes staged policy directives, nonce handling, reporting endpoints, analytics restrictions, and verification steps.
The response recommends allowlisted destinations, identifier-based redirects, rejection of external URLs, and tests for malicious parameters.

best_practices: Provide the framework, rendering path, and trust boundary before asking for implementation advice.
Ask for tests that prove unsafe HTML, URLs, and embedded content are rejected.
Deploy CSP and clickjacking protections gradually with monitoring before enforcement.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on client-side validation as the only security control.
Do not store long-lived secrets in browser storage without a documented threat model.
Do not allow dynamic HTML, scripts, or redirects without strict allowlists.

faq.question: Can this skill fix XSS vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can guide secure rendering changes, sanitization choices, and tests. You still need to apply and verify the changes in your codebase.

faq.question: Does it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on frontend implementation guidance and targeted reviews, not full compliance or penetration testing.

faq.question: Can it help with Content Security Policy?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers CSP directives, nonce and hash strategies, reporting, and staged deployment.

faq.question: Does it support specific frontend frameworks?
faq.answer: The guidance is framework-neutral. Provide your framework details for more specific recommendations.

faq.question: Can it review third-party scripts and widgets?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest SRI, sandboxing, consent boundaries, and safer integration patterns.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static findings were false positives, and no malicious behavior was found in SKILL.md.
